title: Parent-Root Handoff Residue Follow-On Task Split Authoring
objective: Convert the next remaining provenance-routing residue after Packets 169 through 172 into separately executable follow-on packets so the still-current-looking 2026-04-22 parent-root publication/checkpoint handoff family and reevaluation handoff family can be normalized in parallel without collapsing into a broad archive rewrite.
dependency_note: Packets 106 through 168 demoted the strongest current-looking parent-root routing and draft-publication residue one explicit family at a time, and Packets 169 through 172 then closed the bounded AI backbone lane separately. The next adjacent residue is the older 2026-04-22 parent-root handoff family outside the already-demoted draft-publication tranche, so the smallest truthful move is to author concrete follow-on packets for the two remaining handoff families rather than reopen the archive as one undifferentiated cleanup lane.
execution_scope: Author a ready packet spec for the remaining 2026-04-22 parent-root publication and checkpoint handoff family.
Author a ready packet spec for the remaining 2026-04-22 parent-root reevaluation handoff family.
Update repo-owned status routing so the split is explicit and later execution does not recombine the families by accident.
forbidden_scope: execution of the publication/checkpoint handoff packet itself
execution of the reevaluation handoff packet itself
success_criteria: The repo has one ready packet spec for the remaining parent-root publication/checkpoint handoff family.
The repo has one ready packet spec for the remaining parent-root reevaluation handoff family.
Project status routing makes the split explicit and preserves the rule that the two families stay separate execution slices.
validation_commands: Select-String -Path PROJECT_STATUS.md -Pattern 'Packet `2026-05-08-olares-dev-residency-173` now converts the next remaining provenance-routing residue into two separately executable follow-on packets'
next_action: Execute Packet 174 for the remaining parent-root publication/checkpoint handoff family or Packet 175 for the remaining parent-root reevaluation handoff family as separate slices; if both run, keep them in separate validation loops and commits.
